_comment: v4.19.0 parse-disambiguation test set. Each case is an ambiguous Kazakh token where the FST returns 2+ parses; the gold-correct root is the one that fits the surrounding sentence context. Used by `eval_parse_disambiguation` binary to measure how often each prior strategy (lexicographic baseline / unigram / bigram / smoothed / POS-conditioned) picks the gold parse. Hand-labeled by author; bias the cases toward the actually-troublesome surface forms surfaced by past live-REPL transcripts (v3.9.5 неліктен → нелік bug, v4.3.5 онда → он, v4.3.5 жаңа, v4.4.10 қысқасы, v4.4.10 ештеңе) plus cross-domain ambiguous content nouns (тіл, көз, сай).

cases.note: Native nominative noun; body-part reading. Should be unambiguous on this token; included as anti-regression baseline.

cases.sentence: Қазақ тілі — түркі тілдер тобына жатады.
cases.note: Same root as body-part case, but linguistic-domain context. Both readings collapse to the same root in the lexicon — case included to confirm priors don't fragment the parse for linguistic contexts.

v3.9.5 regression: FST returns 3 parses including `нелік + Ablative` (root «нелік»). Pre-fix the lexicographic order picked нелік, dialog replied about location. Test: priors should rank the bare interrogative reading higher because `нелік + Abl` is a vanishingly rare chain.

v4.3.5 regression: gold reading is `ол + Locative` (anaphoric «there» — ол is in the lexicon as part_of_speech=pronoun). v4.20.5 EMPIRICAL DISCOVERY via the eval debug pass: the FST's analyse() returned NO parse with root=ол for онда — only `он + Locative`. v4.21.0 CLOSES THIS by adding the pronoun-paradigm matcher (crates/adam-kernel-fst/src/pronoun_paradigm.rs) — a hardcoded irregular-form table for ол's oblique cases (онда/оны/оның/оған/одан/онымен). With both parses now available, chain_tiebreak_root strategy correctly picks ол via root prior + closed-class boost. RESOLVED at v4.21.0.

cases.sentence: Менің кітабым бар, онда суреттер көп.
cases.note: v4.19.5 companion case to onda_must_not_be_ten_locative. Same ambiguity in principle (он=ten vs ол=anaphoric), mid-sentence position. v4.21.0 RESOLVES via the new pronoun-paradigm matcher: with the gold parse (ол + Loc) now in the candidate list, chain_tiebreak_root picks ол.

v4.4.10 regression: FST returns `қысқа + 3sg-Possessive + Nominative` (root «қысқа» = short). The discourse-particle reading «in short» also produces this root via possessive; the ROOT is correct here (қысқа), but the question is whether the PARSE choice is the discourse-particle reading. Gold = `қысқа` (the only root) — included to verify no regression.
v4.4.10: indefinite-quantifier pronoun `ештеңе`. FST returns it as a noun root. Multiple parses possible if FST tries derivational stems. Gold = `ештеңе`.
Native noun «alma» (apple). Could conflict with imperative «алма» («don't take», from `ал` verb stem + negative). Sentence context is definitional, so noun reading wins. Tests verb/noun ambiguity.
Existential `бар` (= have/exist). Conflicts with verb `бар` (= go) imperative. Existential reading is the canonical noun-like form; verb interpretation produces a different chain.
Verb `кел` (come) imperative. Could conflict with noun `кел` (= raisin). Imperative + locative-goal context favours verb.
Existential negator `жоқ`. FST may parse as noun root. Pre-v4.13.0 `жоқ` was missing from NOT_A_TOPIC; now closed-class. Gold root is `жоқ`.
Noun «тау» (mountain). FST has homonym `тау` as verb root («tighten / contract»). Definitional sentence context favours noun.
Noun «қаза» (grief / death). Could conflict with `Қазақстан` short form (case-marked). Definitional context favours common noun.
Reflexive determiner `өз`. Already in NOT_A_TOPIC at v3.9.5. Verifying lexicographic + priors agree on the closed-class reading.
Common noun «шай» (tea). FST may parse as noun or verb stem (шаю = to wash). Imperative context with «беріңіз» (give) favours object-noun reading.
Noun «күй» (state/mood). Conflicts with verb `күй` (burn). Compound «көңіл-күй» (mood) signals noun reading.
Noun «шөл» (desert/thirst). Multiple senses but same root. Gold = `шөл`.
Noun «ел» (country/people). v4.3.2 had `интеллект → ел` substring bug; the standalone token should always parse as the bare noun.
Noun «күн» (day/sun). Definitional context.
Noun «тас» (stone). Could conflict with verb `тас` (overflow). Subject of intransitive «жатыр» favours noun.
Quantifier «көп» (many/much). Closed-class — should never be a topic noun. Already in NOT_A_TOPIC.

cases.sentence: Менің ойым бар, бұнда шындық бар.
cases.note: v4.21.5 paradigm coverage extension. Anaphoric demonstrative «бұл» (this) in Locative case = бұнда («in this»). Mirrors the онда case at v4.21.0: «бұл» is a pronoun root, the Locative form involves stem alternation `бұл → бұн-` before the consonant-initial -да, and without the v4.21.5 paradigm matcher the FST returned no parse with root=бұл. The mid-sentence position gives the chain_tiebreak_root strategy the prev_chain context, but the actual disambiguation comes from the paradigm matcher making the gold parse available.

v4.21.5 paradigm coverage extension. Dative of demonstrative «сол» (that) = соған («to that»). Stem alternation `сол → соғ-` before the dative -ан suffix. Common in dialog around references / agreements («Сен соған сенесің бе?» = «Do you believe in that?»). Pre-v4.21.5 FST returned no parse with root=сол — the form starts with «соғ», not «сол» as a literal prefix.

v4.21.5 paradigm coverage extension. Dative of personal pronoun «мен» (I) = маған («to me»). Stem alternation `мен → ма-` is the only irregular form in мен's paradigm (other cases — мені/менің/менде/менен — round-trip through regular synth). One of the highest-frequency function words in dialog; «маған көмек керек» = «I need help».

v4.21.5 paradigm coverage extension. Dative of personal pronoun «сен» (you-informal) = саған («to you»). Same `сен → са-` alternation pattern as мен → ма-. Other cases of сен (сені/сенің/сенде/сенен) round-trip through regular synth. «Саған не керек?» = «What do you need?» — common dialog form.
